skill.name: jstodocx-api
skill.description: Generate Microsoft Word DOCX files through the jstodocx.com API. Use when Codex needs to create or download .docx files from basic HTML, JavaScript source text, or controlled document template fields; when a user asks for HTML to DOCX, JS source to Word, or template-based DOCX generation via an API; or when integrating a lightweight Cloudflare Worker DOCX conversion endpoint into scripts, tests, or agent workflows.
skill.summary: Documentation-only skill for generating Word DOCX files via the jstodocx.com REST API. Supports HTML, JavaScript source, and template modes.
skill.author: daanaagua

security_audit.summary: The skill is a documentation-only skill containing no executable code. All 63 static findings are false positives. The Ruby/shell backtick detections are Markdown code blocks showing curl examples for API documentation. Weak cryptography detections refer to the PK ZIP magic bytes for DOCX validation. Hardcoded URLs and API key placeholders (YOUR_API_KEY, $JSTODOCX_API_KEY) are legitimate documentation patterns. The skill explicitly includes safety rules preventing credential exfiltration and arbitrary code execution.

content.user_title: Generate Word DOCX Files via API
content.value_statement: Converting HTML or source code to Word documents requires heavy dependencies. This skill provides a lightweight API-based approach to generate DOCX files without local document-processing libraries.
actual_capabilities: Generate DOCX files from basic HTML (headings, paragraphs, lists, tables, bold, italic, inline code)
Archive JavaScript source code as formatted Word documents with optional line numbers
Create structured documents (reports, invoices, proposals, resumes, meeting notes) from template fields
Authenticate API requests using Bearer token or x-api-key header
Validate generated DOCX files using ZIP magic bytes (PK) and content-type headers
limitations: Only basic HTML formatting is supported; unsafe blocks (scripts, iframes, embeds) are stripped
JavaScript source mode treats code as plain text and never executes it
Pixel-perfect HTML fidelity is not guaranteed by the API
Rate limits apply (typically 30 requests per hour) and aggressive retry loops should be avoided

use_cases.title: Agent Workflow Document Output
use_cases.description: AI agents generate downloadable Word reports or proposals as part of automated workflows without installing document libraries.

use_cases.title: Source Code Archival
use_cases.description: Developers export JavaScript source files into formatted Word documents for code review, documentation, or compliance archival.

use_cases.title: Template-Based Invoice Generation
use_cases.description: Small businesses generate invoices, reports, and meeting notes from structured JSON fields using the template mode.

prompt_templates.title: Basic HTML to DOCX
prompt_templates.prompt:
Convert this HTML to a DOCX file using the jstodocx API:

Project Status

Q4 milestones on track.

  • Feature A shipped
  • Feature B in review
Save as project-status.docx.

prompt_templates.title: Archive JavaScript Source
prompt_templates.prompt: Use jstodocx API js-source mode to archive the file src/utils/parser.js as parser-source.docx with line numbers enabled.

prompt_templates.title: Generate Invoice from Template
prompt_templates.prompt:
Generate an invoice DOCX using jstodocx template mode with these fields:
- heading: Invoice for Acme Corp
- paragraph: Net 30 payment terms
- rows: [[Item, Qty, Amount], [Consulting, 10, $5000]]
- footer: Generated via API

prompt_templates.title: Authenticated API Request
prompt_templates.prompt:
Send the following DOCX generation request with Bearer auth using the JSTODOCX_API_KEY environment variable: Mode: html Title: Confidential Report Content:

Internal Notes

Summary paragraph here.
prompt_templates.scenario: Authenticated requests with environment variable credentials

output_examples.input:
Mode: html, Title: Release Plan, Content:

Release Plan

Hello team.
output_examples.output: Binary DOCX file saved as release-plan.docx. Validated by checking content-type header and PK ZIP magic bytes.

output_examples.input: Mode: js-source, Code: function add(a,b) { return a+b; }, Options: lineNumbers=true
output_examples.output: Formatted Word document with syntax-aware layout and numbered lines. JavaScript is treated as text only and never executed.

output_examples.input: Mode: template, Template: invoice, Fields: heading, paragraph, rows, footer
output_examples.output: Structured invoice document with heading, descriptive paragraph, tabular line items, and footer text.

best_practices: Always verify the DOCX response by checking content-type, filename, and PK ZIP magic bytes before trusting the output
Never print, log, commit, or store API keys; use environment variables like JSTODOCX_API_KEY for authentication
Summarize mode, byte size, and status in logs instead of including raw document content
anti_patterns: Sending protected or confidential content to the API without explicit user confirmation
Looping aggressively on 429 rate limit responses without waiting for the reset window
Treating JavaScript source mode as code execution; it only formats source as text

faq.question: What is the jstodocx API used for?
faq.answer: It converts basic HTML, JavaScript source text, or structured template fields into Microsoft Word DOCX files via a simple HTTP POST request.

faq.question: Does the API execute JavaScript code?
faq.answer: No. The js-source mode treats JavaScript as plain text and formats it into a document with optional line numbers. No code is executed.

faq.question: How is authentication handled?
faq.answer: The endpoint accepts Bearer tokens via the Authorization header or an x-api-key header. API keys should be stored as environment variables.

faq.question: What rate limits apply?
faq.answer: Typical limits are 30 requests per hour. On a 429 response, wait for the reset window before retrying.

faq.question: How can I validate a generated DOCX?
faq.answer: Check the response content-type, save the binary body to a .docx file, and verify it begins with the PK ZIP magic bytes.

faq.question: Does the API support advanced HTML features?
faq.answer: No. Only basic formatting is supported: headings, paragraphs, bold, italic, underline, inline code, lists, preformatted text, and simple tables.
